key_insight: Reinforcement learning can be analyzed as a unified problem of fixed-point operators, stochastic approximation, and optimization, which clarifies convergence, stability, and sample-complexity guarantees.
engineering_relevance: The survey gives practitioners a mathematical toolkit for designing and diagnosing RL algorithms with stronger reliability in value estimation, policy optimization, off-policy learning, and constrained control.
method: Bellman operator theory: contraction, monotonicity, and fixed-point arguments for value/policy iteration and TD methods.
Optimization view: stochastic approximation, martingale analysis, and convex duality linking mirror and proximal methods.
Function approximation and off-policy/CMDP analysis using concentration bounds, stabilization techniques, and error decomposition.
evidence_signal: The abstract summarizes a survey of mathematical frameworks and theoretical results, focusing on finite-sample bounds, convergence rates, and asymptotic behavior rather than a new empirical benchmark.
watch_item: Many guarantees rely on assumptions such as contraction, mixing, or function-class structure, so direct performance may weaken in large-scale nonlinear RL systems.
